senses.definition: having or capable of producing colors
examples: color film
he rented a color television
marvelous color illustrations

senses.definition: a visual attribute of things that results from the light they emit or transmit or reflect
examples: a white color is made up of many different wavelengths of light
the Puritan Period was lacking in color
the characters were delineated with exceptional vividness

senses.definition: the timbre of a musical sound
examples: the recording fails to capture the true color of the original music

senses.definition: an outward or token appearance or form that is deliberately misleading
examples: he hoped his claims would have a semblance of authenticity
he tried to give his falsehood the gloss of moral sanction
the situation soon took on a different color

senses.definition: any material used for its color
examples: she used a different color for the trim

senses.definition: (physics) the characteristic of quarks that determines their role in the strong interaction
examples: each flavor of quarks comes in three colors

senses.definition: the appearance of objects (or light sources) described in terms of a person's perception of their hue and lightness (or brightness) and saturation

examples: The child colored the drawings
Fall colored the trees
colorize black and white film

senses.definition: affect as in thought or feeling
examples: My personal feelings color my judgment in this case
The sadness tinged his life
His political ideas color his lectures
color the walls with paint in warm tones

senses.definition: give a deceptive explanation or excuse for
examples: color a lie

senses.definition: change color, often in an undesired manner
examples: The shirts discolored
